sections.paragraph:
Ἀσιάρχης, ου, ὁ (Strabo 14, 1, 42; ins) Asiarch, plainly equiv. to the ἀρχιερεὺς Ἀσίας (cp. CB I/2, 465 no. 299, where ἀρχιερεῖς is used in the sense ‘Asiarchs’) MPol 12:2 (cp. 21). Many would understand it so also in Ac 19:31 (s. e.g. JMarquardt, Röm. Staatsverwaltung I2 1881, 513ff; Lghtf., Ign. and Pol. III2 1889, 404ff; Ramsay, Bearing 88). But the titles are sometimes differentiated (SIG 900, 5), and the pl. in Ac rather favors a ref. to deputies of the κοινὸν Ἀσίας, the assembly of Asia, which met in Ephesus (so finally Beyer; Bauernfeind). Cp. Brandis, Pauly-W. II 1564ff (lit.); JWeiss RE X 538f; Thieme 17; LTaylor: Beginn. I 5, ’33, 256–62; DMagie, Roman Rule in Asia Minor, ’50, 449f, 1298–1301, 1526; Haenchen, ad loc.—RKearsley, in: BAFCS II, ’94, 363–76; idem, New Docs 4, 46–55. DDD 1350. EDNT. M-M.

Strabo Strabo , I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

equiv. equiv. = equivalent

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

CB Ramsay, CB = The Cities and Bishoprics of Phrygia

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

Lghtf. Lghtf. = JBLightfoot

Ign. Ign. = Ignatius; always simply I. when cited with one of his epistles

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

Ramsay, Bearing Ramsay, Bearing = The Bearing of Recent Discovery on the Trustworthiness of the NT

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

  1. pl. = plural

ref. ref. = reference(s)

Pauly-W Pauly-W = AP./GW., Real-Encyclopädie der klassischen Altertumswissenschaft—List 6

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

RE RE = Realencyclopädie für protestantische Theologie und Kirche—List 6

Thieme Thieme = GT., Die Inschriften von Magnesia am Mäander und das NT—List 3

Beginn. Beginn. = The Beginnings of Christianity, ed. FJFoakesJackson and KLake—List 6

Haenchen Haenchen = EH., Commentary on Acts—List 6

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

BAFCS BAFCS = The Book of Acts in Its First Century Setting—List 6

idem idem = the same (in ref. to the person just mentioned)

DDD DDD = Dictionary of Deities and Demons in the Bible—List 6

EDNT EDNT = Exegetical Dictionary of the NT—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 143
